merger_name: American Industrial Partners – Honeywell’s Warehouse and Workflow Solutions business

acquirers.name: AIPCF VIII Global Corp Holding L.P.

anzsic_codes.name: Lifting and Material Handling Equipment Manufacturing
Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
Certain affiliates of AIP, LLC. (together, **AIP**) propose to acquire all of the outstanding equity interests in the Warehouse and Workflow Solutions (**WWS**) business of Honeywell International, Inc. (**Honeywell**) (the **Acquisition**).

Honeywell’s WWS business is comprised of Ibiza Holdings, Inc and Honeywell Intelligrated GmbH and their respective subsidiaries (together, **Honeywell WWS**).

AIP is an operationally oriented middle-market private equity firm based in New York, US, with a focus on the acquisition and improvement of industrial businesses. As part of the Acquisition, AIP has established wholly owned special-purpose vehicles which will be the direct acquirers of the equity in Honeywell WWS.

Honeywell is a US-based publicly traded multinational conglomerate headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, United States of America. Honeywell primarily operates in four business areas: (i) Aerospace Technologies, (ii) Building Automation, (iii) Energy and Sustainability Solutions, and (iv) Industrial Automation.

Honeywell WWS is headquartered in Mason, Ohio (U.S) and is a provider of material handling and warehouse automation solutions.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Certain affiliates of AIP, LLC. (together, AIP) propose to acquire all of
the outstanding equity interests in the Warehouse and Workflow
Solutions (WWS) business of Honeywell International, Inc.
(Honeywell), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).
Honeywell’s WWS business is comprised of Ibiza Holdings, Inc and
Honeywell Intelligrated GmbH and their respective subsidiaries
(together, Honeywell WWS).
AIPCF VIII Global Corp Holding L.P. applied for a notification waiver
in respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, AIP, is an operationally oriented middle-market private
equity firm based in New York, US, with a focus on the acquisition
and improvement of industrial businesses. As part of the Acquisition,
AIP has established wholly owned special-purpose vehicles which
will be the direct acquirers of the equity in Honeywell WWS.
The target, Honeywell WWS, is headquartered in Mason, Ohio (U.S)
and is a provider of material handling and warehouse automation
solutions. Honeywell WWS is currently owned by Honeywell, which is
a US-based publicly traded multinational conglomerate
headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, United States of America.
Honeywell primarily operates in four business areas: (i) Aerospace
Technologies, (ii) Building Automation, (iii) Energy and Sustainability
Solutions, and (iv) Industrial Automation.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no overlap between AIP, its connected entities and
Honeywell WWS in the supply of material handling and
automated warehouse solutions in Australia
b. there are alternative suppliers of material handling and
automated warehouse solutions in Australia
c. Honeywell WWS has a small presence in Australia.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
